findings.description: Three tasks modify libs.versions.toml. Task 2 (Wave 2, no deps) adds protobuf/datastore entries. Task 3 (Wave 2, depends_on=[2]) adds robolectric — correctly declared sequential. Task 5 (Wave 3, depends_on=[2,3]) adds camerax/compose-richtext/lifecycle entries — runs only after Wave 2 completes. The chain 2→3→5 is enforced by depends_on and wave ordering.
findings.verdict: OK — sequential ownership is clear and enforced

findings.description: Task 5 (Wave 3) is the owner/creator of strings.xml Phase-2 entries. Tasks 7, 8, 9, 10, 11 are all Wave 4 and sequential (7→8→9→10→11 via depends_on chain). Each Wave 4 task adds its own domain strings without overlap: Task 7 adds snackbar/content-desc for attachments; Task 8 adds camera button/snackbar strings; Task 9 adds audio timer/stop/permission strings; Task 10 adds ThinkingBlock labels; Task 11 adds InferenceSettings/Dialog strings. No two Wave 4 tasks list the same specific strings.
findings.verdict: OK — sequential Wave 4 chain prevents conflicts; string domains are distinct per task

findings.description: ChatScreen.kt is modified by Tasks 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, all Wave 4 sequential. Each task adds a distinct concern: Task 7 integrates MultimodalInputBar+ThumbnailStrip; Task 8 adds CameraBottomSheet trigger; Task 9 adds mic button + AudioRecorderBottomSheet trigger; Task 10 removes private MessageBubble composable and imports the new one; Task 11 adds TopAppBar buttons + autoscroll. The sequential depends_on chain (7→8→9→10→11) ensures each task sees the previous task's changes.
findings.verdict: OK — all modifications are additive and sequential; no parallel writes

findings.description: Task 7 adds: attachments StateFlow, addImages, addImageBitmap, removeAttachment. Task 9 adds: addAudio method. Task 10 adds: thinking accumulation in resultListener, guard flags. Task 11 adds: full state machines for apply*/reset, heavy reinit sequencing. Sequential chain 7→8→9→10→11 is declared. Task 8 does NOT modify ChatViewModel (only ChatScreen + new CameraBottomSheet file) — correctly delegates bitmap to viewModel.addImage(bitmap) declared in Task 7.
findings.verdict: OK — each task adds orthogonal functionality to ChatViewModel

findings.description: Task 1 (Wave 1) adds defaultSystemPrompt parameter to createLlmChatConfigs. Task 10 (Wave 4) adds ConfigKeys.SYSTEM_PROMPT_DEFAULT and extends createLlmChatConfigs with the same parameter. Both tasks describe the same operation. However, the tasks are in different waves (Wave 1 vs Wave 4) with a long sequential chain between them (Wave 1→2→3→4), so Task 10 will always execute after Task 1.
findings.verdict: POTENTIAL OVERLAP — Tasks 1 and 10 both describe adding 'defaultSystemPrompt' to createLlmChatConfigs and Config.kt. If Task 1 fully implements this, Task 10 must not re-add it but instead reference the existing ConfigKey. This needs careful coordination during execution: Task 10 executor must read Task 1 output first and skip any already-implemented steps.
findings.recommendation: Add an explicit note in Task 10 that the createLlmChatConfigs parameter was already introduced in Task 1. The executor of Task 10 should verify what Task 1 actually implemented and only add the missing pieces (ConfigKeys enum entry, ModelAllowlist.kt parsing). Consider splitting Task 10 responsibilities: core-runtime changes belong conceptually to Task 1, UI/ViewModel changes to Task 10.

findings.description: Task 1 modifies ModelAllowlist.kt to add llmSupport* field mapping. Task 10 also modifies ModelAllowlist.kt to parse 'defaultSystemPrompt' and pass to createLlmChatConfigs. These are different fields — no functional overlap. Wave ordering (1=Wave1, 10=Wave4) ensures Task 10 executes after Task 1.
findings.verdict: OK — different fields, no functional conflict, wave ordering ensures sequencing

findings.description: Only Task 3 modifies ErrorLog.kt: adds whitelist enforcement (require check), adds 4 new components ('attachment-decode', 'audio', 'camera', 'settings-io'), changes cause-chain bounding to 200 chars. No other task modifies this file. Tasks 8 (ErrorLog.e 'camera') and 9 (ErrorLog.e 'audio') are consumers — they depend on the whitelist entries added by Task 3.
findings.verdict: OK — single owner (Task 3), consumers in Wave 4 depend on Wave 2 completion via task chain

findings.description: Wave 1 parallel tasks: Task 1 and Task 4. Task 1 modifies: ModelAllowlist.kt, Config.kt, AllowlistLoaderTest.kt, model_allowlist_fixture.json, model_allowlist.json. Task 4 modifies: LlmChatModelHelper.kt (creates MultimodalContentsBuilder.kt + test). Zero file overlap between Tasks 1 and 4.
findings.verdict: OK — no parallel write conflicts in Wave 1

findings.description: Audit Wave tasks (12, 13, 14) all depend_on=[11] and are read-only audits. They produce report files in separate log directories. No shared write targets.
findings.verdict: OK — audit tasks are read-only and write to distinct paths

findings.description: Task 8 (CameraBottomSheet) uses rotateBitmap from MediaUtils (created in Task 3) and addImageBitmap from ChatViewModel (created in Task 7). Task 8's depends_on=[7], which transitively covers Task 3 via 7→5→[2,3]. However, the depends_on chain is: 8→7→[5,6]→[2,3]. Task 7 depends on [5,6], Task 5 depends on [2,3]. So Task 3 IS transitively covered. But this is an implicit transitive dependency — it is not visible from Task 8's declared depends_on.
findings.verdict: ACCEPTABLE — transitive dependency is covered by the wave structure, but the rotateBitmap usage from core-runtime (Task 3) should be noted as a cross-module dependency, not just a task dependency

findings.description: Task 10 uses SafeMarkdown (created in Task 6) for ThinkingBlock. Task 10's dependency chain is 10→9→8→7→[5,6], so Task 6 IS covered. The task description notes this explicitly: 'SafeMarkdown — создаётся в Task 6 (зависимость: Task 10 → 9 → 8 → 7 → 6, поэтому доступен)'. No hidden dependency issue.
findings.verdict: OK — explicitly documented in task description

findings.description: Task 6 notes that app/src/test/ directory does not exist and testImplementation block is missing from app/build.gradle.kts. Task 6 must create this test infrastructure. Task 11 also creates test files in app/src/test/. If Task 6 handles the infrastructure setup, Task 11 (which comes later in Wave 4) will benefit from it. This is correctly sequential.
findings.verdict: OK — Task 6 is responsible for test infrastructure bootstrapping; Task 11 is downstream

findings.description: AudioRecord is created only in Task 9 (AudioRecorderBottomSheet). CameraX ProcessCameraProvider is bound only in Task 8 (CameraBottomSheet). DataStore is initialized only in Task 2 (CoreSettingsModule Hilt singleton). No duplicate infrastructure initialization found.
findings.verdict: OK — each infrastructure component has exactly one owner

findings.description: Permission handling: Task 8 (camera) and Task 9 (audio) both use the same pattern: rememberLauncherForActivityResult → denied snackbar → permanently denied snackbar with Settings deeplink. This is consistent.
findings.verdict: OK — permission handling approach is consistent across Task 8 and Task 9

findings.description: Markdown rendering: Task 6 creates SafeMarkdown+SafeUriHandler. Task 10 uses SafeMarkdown in ThinkingBlock. Task 6 also uses SafeMarkdown in AboutScreen. No task uses raw Markdown rendering bypassing SafeMarkdown. Consistent.
findings.verdict: OK — SafeMarkdown is the single Markdown rendering path

findings.description: Task 6 declares depends_on=[5] (Wave 3 infrastructure). Task 7 declares depends_on=[5, 6]. In Wave 4, Task 7 is the first code task and it correctly depends on both Task 5 (strings) and Task 6 (build infrastructure for test). However Task 6 and Task 7 are both Wave 4. Looking at the wave metadata: Task 6 wave=4 depends_on=[5], Task 7 wave=4 depends_on=[5,6]. Within Wave 4, Tasks 6→7→8→9→10→11 form a strict sequential chain despite all being labeled wave=4. This is correct — depends_on enforces the sequential order within the same wave label.
findings.verdict: OK — within-wave sequential ordering is correctly enforced via depends_on chain

findings.description: Task 13 (Security Audit) wave is labeled 'audit' while Task 12 is labeled '5' and Task 14 is labeled 'audit'. Minor inconsistency in wave label naming between Tasks 12 and 13/14, but all three have depends_on=[11] and are read-only audits that can safely run in parallel. The 'audit' vs '5' label is cosmetic and does not affect execution logic.
findings.verdict: MINOR — Task 12 labels wave as '5', Tasks 13/14 label it 'audit'. No functional impact since all three depend on Task 11 and are parallel audits. Recommend normalizing to 'audit' for clarity.

findings.description: Both Task 1 and Task 10 describe adding defaultSystemPrompt parsing in ModelAllowlist.kt and the createLlmChatConfigs parameter. Task 1 Details section says: 'Config.kt — createLlmChatConfigs(...): добавить параметр defaultSystemPrompt'. Task 10 Details section says: 'Config.kt — добавить SYSTEM_PROMPT_DEFAULT ConfigKey, расширить createLlmChatConfigs'. The descriptions are not identical (Task 1 adds the parameter; Task 10 adds the named ConfigKey), but if Task 1 already emits the ConfigKey entry (as stated: 'эмитировать LabelConfig(SYSTEM_PROMPT_DEFAULT, defaultSystemPrompt)'), then Task 10 may be duplicating work. The distinction is: Task 1 references SYSTEM_PROMPT_DEFAULT as a label string, Task 10 adds it as a typed ConfigKeys enum entry.
findings.verdict: RISK — potential double-implementation of SYSTEM_PROMPT_DEFAULT. Executor of Task 10 must carefully diff what Task 1 actually produced. Recommendation: Task 1 should define the full ConfigKey; Task 10 should only wire it into awaitInitialize and accumulate thinking.

summary: All 15 tasks reviewed. The dependency graph is sound: Wave 1 (Tasks 1, 4) has no file conflicts; Wave 2 is correctly sequential (2→3); Wave 3 (Task 5) depends on Wave 2 completion; Wave 4 (Tasks 6→7→8→9→10→11) forms a strict sequential chain via depends_on; Audit Wave (Tasks 12, 13, 14) are read-only and parallel; Task 15 (final) depends on all audits. The most significant finding (F-05, F-18) is a POTENTIAL DOUBLE-IMPLEMENTATION between Task 1 and Task 10: both describe adding defaultSystemPrompt to Config.kt and ModelAllowlist.kt. This is not a dependency graph error (Task 10 always runs after Task 1 due to wave ordering), but the executor of Task 10 must verify what Task 1 actually implemented and skip already-done work. A minor cosmetic inconsistency exists in wave label naming (Task 12 uses '5', Tasks 13/14 use 'audit') with no functional impact. All other shared resources have clear single owners and correct sequential or wave-ordered access. Status: APPROVED with the Config.kt overlap risk noted for execution-time attention.
